2014-10-01.title: In a Shift, British Sharpen Their Tone on Hong Kong Protests

2014-10-01.abstract: The pro-democracy protesters have occupied parts of Hong Kong for more than three weeks.

2014-10-01.title: Seeking New Heights in France, National Front Party Thinks Locally

2014-10-01.title: For Obama and Indian Leader, a Friendly Stroll if Not a Full Embrace
2014-10-01.abstract: The White House tried to spotlight hopes for working with Prime Minister Narendra Modi while not lavishing full pageantry on a leader who was once barred from entering the United States.

2014-10-01.title: Chinese Web Censors Struggle With Hong Kong Protest
2014-10-01.abstract: Beijing’s effort to limit information on the Hong Kong protests has been met by clever ways of expressing dissent.

2014-10-02.title: Egypt Seizes Newspapers to Censor an Article

2014-10-02.title: Canada: Shipwreck in Arctic Identified
2014-10-02.abstract: The wreck of a ship discovered last month after being trapped in Arctic ice in the 1840s has been identified as the H.M.S. Erebus.

2014-10-03.title: Mideast Virus MERS Merits Caution, Not Alarm, W.H.O. Says
2014-10-03.abstract: The announcement coincided with the annual Muslim pilgrimage to Mecca. The report suggested precautions for travelers to Saudi Arabia to minimize risk of exposure to MERS.

2014-10-03.title: Hong Kong Protesters Divided Over Use of Traffic as a Weapon
2014-10-03.abstract: Despite appeals from their young leaders, some protesters in Hong Kong tried to escalate their clash with the authorities by blocking a main road.

2014-10-03.title: Turkey Votes to Allow Operations Against ISIS
2014-10-03.abstract: Parliament also voted Thursday to allow foreign forces to launch operations from Turkey, but it was far from clear if the political step would soon translate into military action.

2014-10-03.title: Some Hong Kong Residents, Weary of Disruptions, Find Fault With Protesters’ Methods
2014-10-03.abstract: The mostly young people of the so-called Umbrella Revolution have found uneven support among middle-age and older people.

2014-10-03.abstract: The American promise to build 18 Ebola treatment centers has been stalled by, among other things, broken equipment and numerous holes on an airport runway.

2014-10-04.abstract: Satellite data shows that Brazil’s annual deforestation rate in the Amazon is rising again after years of declines.

2014-10-04.title: In Unusual Trip, North Korean Aides Attend Games in South
2014-10-04.abstract: Three trusted aides to the North Korean leader Kim Jong-un arrived in South Korea on Saturday to attend the closing ceremony of the Asian Games in a highly unusual trip.

2014-10-04.abstract: In its third commentary in three days on the Hong Kong protests, the Chinese Communist Party mouthpiece warned Saturday of “turmoil” if the rule of law is not respected.

2014-10-05.title: A Cease-Fire in Doubt in Ukraine
2014-10-05.abstract: Civilian casualties mounted as the Ukrainian Army and separatist rebels continued to exchange fire in their battle for control of the Donetsk airport.

2014-10-05.title: In Ukraine, Civilians in Crossfire
2014-10-05.abstract: Civilian casualties have soared as separatist rebels with heavy artillery attack the airport in Donetsk and Ukrainian forces respond with often ill-directed fire.

2014-10-05.abstract: Photographs shared on Twitter from the pro-democracy demonstrations in Hong Hong.

2014-10-06.abstract: Despite a bold speech at the Conservatives’ party convention, the British prime minister still faces a tough road to re-election next year.

2014-10-07.title: Senate Candidate in Brazil in Hiding After Slapping Voter on Election Day
2014-10-07.abstract: A retired police officer who failed to win a seat in Brazil’s Senate seized the nation’s attention by slapping a voter at a polling site.

2014-10-07.abstract: The vice president’s problems began Thursday when he declared that the biggest problem in dealing with Syria and the rise of the Islamic State was America’s allies in the region.

2014-10-08.abstract: Prosecutors called for the conviction and life imprisonment of Radovan Karadzic on 11 charges, including genocide, stemming from the 1992-95 war.

2014-10-08.abstract: A speech to the United Nations General Assembly burnished President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi’s image at home, where the former general builds power on Egyptians’ desire to avoid the chaos nearby.

2014-10-08.abstract: Passengers could have their temperature checked or be subject to detailed questioning, but health officials cautioned against more draconian measures like travel bans.

2014-10-08.abstract: Health workers tend to people sickened by Ebola and those quarantined while they wait to learn if they have the virus.

2014-10-09.abstract: Europe has suffered a blow to its self-image of generosity, its efforts to contain Ebola overshadowed  by President Obama’s announcement that he was sending  3,000 troops to West Africa.

2014-10-09.abstract: Officials warned that airstrikes alone would not be sufficient to protect the Syrian town of Kobani from the Islamic State fighters.

2014-10-10.abstract: Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif’s trip to North Waziristan was a show of support for the military and an effort to improve relations with its generals.

2014-10-10.abstract: Britain said it would introduce measures at airports and rail terminals to screen passengers from affected countries as concerns over Ebola grew in Europe.

2014-10-10.abstract: With thousands infected with the Ebola virus, millions of dollars are being pledged to develop treatment options. Three avenues that have potential but are a long way from being viable treatments.

2014-10-10.abstract: The presidents of Guinea, Liberia and Sierra Leone, the nations most affected by the Ebola outbreak, made their case at a meeting of the World Bank and the International Monetary Fund in Washington.

2014-10-10.abstract: South Korean prosecutors have charged Tatsuya Kato, the former Seoul bureau chief for the Japanese newspaper Sankei Shimbun, with defaming the South Korean president.

2014-10-11.abstract: In the growing crop of tell-alls by former Obama officials, Hillary Rodham Clinton has emerged largely unscathed — proof that it is easier to kick a sitting second-term president than a potential future one.

2014-10-11.abstract: Mr. Satyarthi, a co-winner the 2014 Nobel Peace Prize, has labored over India’s numbingly huge problem of child slavery, and his organization is credited with freeing some 70,000 of them.

2014-10-11.abstract: The Islamic State is pouring reinforcements into the besieged Syrian city of Kobani, a Kurdish enclave, despite heavy attacks from American-led aircraft.

2014-10-11.abstract: The decision to help families treat patients at home signifies a significant shift in the struggle against the rampaging disease.

2014-10-12.abstract: An international donor conference in Cairo on reconstructing Gaza garnered $5.4 billion in pledges, but it came short of the $8.5 billion that Mahmoud Abbas of the Palestinian Authority had requested.

2014-10-12.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: One or several German aircraft bombarded Paris in 1914.

2014-10-14.abstract: This phase of his trial permits both sides to call witnesses offering testimonials to perhaps sway the judge in determining the punishment.

2014-10-14.abstract: The move came a day after three men were detained in London, part of a series of arrests since Britain raised its terrorism threat assessment in August.

2014-10-15.abstract: Rula Ghani will advocate for women and children, taking on far more prominence than her predecessor, Zeenat Karzai, who was rarely seen in public.

2014-10-16.abstract: From Moscow to Caracas, Riyadh to Baghdad, in Tehran, Algiers, Kuwait City and Lagos, officials are confronting the plunge in prices — roughly 25 percent since a peak in June.

2014-10-16.abstract: After his acquittal from the phone hacking scandal, Charles Brooks sought to be reimbursed by the government for the legal costs of his defense.

2014-10-16.abstract: As Iraq fights the Islamic State, Prime Minister Haider al-Abadi is also battling critics in his own party and the Shiite bloc it leads.

2014-10-16.abstract: In an effort to prop up prices, officials have signaled a willingness to endure reduced profits rather than cut production, as they did to their later regret in the early 1980s.

2014-10-17.abstract: Aleksei A. Navalny, an anticorruption blogger, antagonized some of his fellow liberals by appearing to rule out the possibility of simply returning Crimea to Ukraine.

2014-10-17.abstract: International fast-food chains and high-end clubs are racing to get established in the fast-growing Kenyan capital, and the staff at some new spots are a novelty.

2014-10-17.abstract: A problem with his Air Force jet prompted Secretary of State John Kerry to rush to the airport Thursday morning for a commercial flight back to Washington.

2014-10-18.abstract: Islamic State militants are still dominating the fight in Iraq’s crucial Anbar Province weeks into the American air campaign.

2014-10-19.abstract: Residents of Iguala, Mexico, searching for students who disappeared after a clash with the police, have not found them, but they have found mass graves.

2014-10-19.abstract: More than 1,000 birch-bark documents have been uncovered after being preserved for hundreds of years in the magical mud of Veliky Novgorod.

2014-10-19.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: Turkey provided an Eastern access route to the Allies in 1938.

2014-10-19.abstract: Four men with knives and explosives targeted the police and merchants at a market in Xinjiang, Radio Free Asia said.

2014-10-19.abstract: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s party won the most seats in two important Indian state elections where votes were counted on Sunday.

2014-10-20.abstract: China is in the midst of a scorching campaign against government corruption, but its system of investigation often operates in secret and beyond the law, leading to abuses suffered by the officials being targeted.

2014-10-20.abstract: President Dilma Rousseff is in a tough fight for the Oct. 26 election after a former executive at Petrobras took a plea bargain and testified that her party had benefited from a bribery scheme.

2014-10-20.abstract: Almost four years after the uprising that overthrew President Zine el-Abidine Ben Ali and set off the Arab Spring, frustration in Tunisia is such that people often say they wish for a return of the Ben Ali era.

2014-10-20.abstract: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, 26, tried to reclaim the legacy of his mother with a huge rally in the southern port city of Karachi.

2014-10-20.title: Historic Loss May Follow Rise of Rents in Barcelona
2014-10-20.abstract: After 20 years’ warning of an impending rent increase, there is 11th-hour resistance to small shops’ being pushed from historic districts by an inundation of international brands.

2014-10-21.title: U.S. Denial of Visas for 6 in Hungary Strains Ties
2014-10-21.abstract: A diplomatic incident involving unnamed public officials has transfixed Hungarian political circles and provided fresh evidence of tension between the two nations.

2014-10-21.abstract: A Belfast woman says that she was raped by a man she accuses of being a senior member of the I.R.A., and that Gerry Adams, the Sinn Fein leader, helped cover it up.

2014-10-21.abstract: A veteran diplomat denied North Korea had prison camps and warned against any effort to charge the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un, with crimes against humanity.

2014-10-21.abstract: José Manuel Barroso, the departing president of the European Commission, said Britain’s confrontational stance with European Union states is hurting its chances of winning the policy changes it wants.

2014-10-22.title: Student Spins Double Life Among Spanish Elite
2014-10-22.abstract: Security agents were left puzzled over how Francisco Nicolás Gómez Iglesias, 20, was able to broker business deals, lunch with politicians, and shake hands with the king.

2014-10-22.abstract: Ebola is wiping away the small gains made in war-scarred parts of West Africa, potentially threatening the hard-won stability in a tinderbox part of the world.

2014-10-22.abstract: A Polish man paralyzed from the chest down can use a walker and has some leg sensation after a novel treatment, a report says, but some experts warn against premature conclusions.

2014-10-22.abstract: Several candidates have been assaulted in recent days, highlighting the turmoil surrounding a vote that will complete an overhaul of the government.

2014-10-22.abstract: Even as Tunisians have approved a new constitution and the country has one of the Arab world’s most educated populations, militants are recruiting more openly there than in any other country.

2014-10-23.title: Estonia: Tensions Over a Russian Jet
2014-10-23.abstract: A Russian military surveillance airplane crossed into Estonia’s airspace and was intercepted by NATO jets, the Estonian Foreign Ministry’s spokeswoman said.

2014-10-23.abstract: A tradition of herding has continued for centuries in western China, despite official efforts to force nomads to settle in villages. But a recent drought has put that lifestyle to the test.

2014-10-23.title: Presidential Challenger in Brazil Forced to Carry On Without Lindsay Lohan’s Support
2014-10-23.abstract: A unusual Twitter message posted in the actress’s name was pulled after stirring suspicions, and mockery.

2014-10-23.abstract: Demonstrations erupted after assaults that coincided with the passage of a law designed to protect those who correct people deemed to be acting in an “un-Islamic” way.

2014-10-23.abstract: The attorney general says that the apparent abduction of 43 students in southern Mexico came after Mayor Jose Luis Abarca of Iguala ordered an attack on them.

2014-10-24.title: Unable to Marry Gay Couples, Some Italian Mayors Rebel
2014-10-24.abstract: Local leaders aim to force national legislators to clarify the legal standing of same-sex couples who have married abroad.

2014-10-24.title: In Iraq, Relief After News of Blackwater Convictions
2014-10-24.abstract: For many of the survivors of the 2007 shooting in Baghdad, the verdicts offered some solace after a traumatic event that became synonymous with the worst of the American occupation.

2014-10-24.title: Jihadist Captive Faces Terror Charges in U.S.
2014-10-24.abstract: The former Russian soldier who joined mujahedeen fighters during Russia’s war in Afghanistan in 1980s also later fought against American troops until his capture in 2009.

2014-10-24.abstract: Dr. Keiji Fukuda, an assistant director-general for the World Health Organization, said that the situation in Guinea, Liberia and Sierra Leone was still of “great concern.”

2014-10-24.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron of Britain said his country had already promised more aid than any other in Europe to fight Ebola in West Africa, but said, “we need other European countries to do more.”

2014-10-25.title: Turkey: MERS Virus Case Is Reported
2014-10-25.abstract: The potentially fatal MERS virus that originated in Saudi Arabia two years ago has spread to Turkey for the first time, the World Health Organization said Friday.

2014-10-25.title: Pakistan: Court Acquits a Suspect Charged in the Murder of Daniel Pearl
2014-10-25.abstract: A Pakistani court on Friday acquitted one of the main suspects in the 2002 murder of the American journalist Daniel Pearl.

2014-10-25.title: Putin Accuses U.S. of Backing ‘Neo-Fascists’ and ‘Islamic Radicals’
2014-10-25.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia unleashed perhaps his strongest diatribe against the United States yet.

2014-10-25.title: With Stunts and Vigilante Escapades, a Populist Gains Ground in Ukraine
2014-10-25.abstract: Oleh Lyashko, the Radical Party leader, appears poised to gain a significant bloc in Ukraine’s Parliament in Sunday’s elections, tapping into voters’ frustrations over war and a bad economy.

2014-10-25.title: Islamist Party in Tunisia Appears Set to Rebound
2014-10-25.abstract: After ceding power to a caretaker government in January, Ennahda enters Sunday’s national legislative elections as a front-runner.

2014-10-26.title: 1939: Ribbentrop Will Bear Guilt for Tragedy of War, Neville Chamberlain Says
2014-10-26.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: Neville Chamberlain blames Ribbentrop for choosing war over peace in 1939.

2014-10-26.title: Pro-Democracy Movement’s Vote in Hong Kong Abruptly Called Off
2014-10-26.abstract: The cancellation of the balloting, which had been intended as a display of unity, exposed tensions over how to sustain the protests.

2014-10-26.title: Want to Touch a Dog? In Malaysia, It’s a Delicate Subject
2014-10-26.abstract: The backlash by conservative Muslims over an attempt to widen appreciation of dogs has underlined the fault lines between Muslims and other groups in Malaysia.

2014-10-26.title: 1964: Future of Concorde in Question as Britain Asks for Project Review
2014-10-26.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: A review threatens the development of the Concorde in 1964.

2014-10-26.title: Brazil Stays With Rousseff as President After Turbulent Campaign
2014-10-26.abstract: Dilma Rousseff defeated a centrist challenger, Aécio Neves, in a tumultuous race marked by accusations of corruption, personal insults and heated debates.

2014-10-27.title: Peace Prize Laureates Urge Disclosure on U.S. Torture
2014-10-27.abstract: Twelve of President Obama’s fellow Nobel Peace Prize recipients are pushing him to release a Senate report about the C.I.A.'s use of torture after the Sept. 11 attacks.

2014-10-27.title: Conservative Seems Likely to Reach Second Presidential Round in Uruguay
2014-10-27.abstract: Luis Lacalle Pou, 41, a leader of the conservative National Party, has mounted a strong challenge to Tabaré Vázquez, a leader of the governing Broad Front.

2014-10-27.title: Iraqi Christian Refugees in Jordan
2014-10-27.abstract: About 4,000 Christians left the Iraqi city of Mosul for Jordan in the last three months, forced out by Islamic State fighters.

2014-10-27.title: In Wales, a Toast to Dylan Thomas on His 100th Birthday
2014-10-27.abstract: In a country that has long been ill at ease with its hard-living son, Thomas’s granddaughter is trying to refocus public attention on the poet’s work.

2014-10-27.title: In Video, Ottawa Gunman Spoke of Political Motives
2014-10-27.abstract: While the police did not release the video, an official said it provided persuasive evidence that the attack was motivated by the gunman’s ideology.

2014-10-28.title: Brazil Vote Highlights a Rift Linked to Economics
2014-10-28.abstract: President Dilma Rousseff, who was re-elected on Sunday, has signaled changes that could bring a shift away from policies that have created anger in Brazil’s business establishment.

2014-10-28.title: A Capital of the Arts Is Forced to Evolve
2014-10-28.abstract: Economic troubles are clouding Paris’s role as a cultural beacon as France shifts from state funding and management of the arts toward a greater role by the private sector.

2014-10-28.title: Wish to Do More in Ebola Fight Meets Reality in Liberia
2014-10-28.abstract: Health workers in Liberia and elsewhere in West Africa are finding they have to ration care, operating under constraints they often find frustrating.

2014-10-28.title: Canada: Terrorism Bill Unveiled
2014-10-28.abstract: The government of Prime Minister Stephen Harper introduced an antiterrorism bill on Monday that was to have been brought before lawmakers the day a gunman stormed Parliament.

2014-10-28.title: Ford Family Loses Hold on City Hall in Toronto
2014-10-28.abstract: John Tory, a center-right candidate for mayor, defeated Mayor Rob Ford’s brother, who had replaced him on the ballot.

2014-10-29.title: Despite Rumors, Not Everything That Towers Is Eiffel’s
2014-10-29.abstract: In parts of Peru and Chile, famed engineer Gustave Eiffel’s name is linked to many structures he had nothing to do with.

2014-10-29.title: Echoes of Patty Hearst in Kobani
2014-10-29.abstract: Narration by the British hostage John Cantlie on Islamic State propaganda videos is reminiscent of recordings by the kidnapped Patty Hearst.

2014-10-29.title: Computing the Political Impact of Canada’s Attacks
2014-10-29.abstract: After two attacks by homegrown extremists, the questions now are about the impact on Prime Minister Stephen Harper’s future.

2014-10-29.title: In Liberia, a Good or Very Bad Sign: Empty Hospital Beds
2014-10-29.abstract: Liberia has far fewer people being treated for Ebola than anticipated, but health officials are hesitant to declare victory.

2014-10-29.title: 21 Arrested Over Deadly Land Clash in Southwest China
2014-10-29.abstract: Those arrested included six employees of a construction firm and 15 residents of Jinning County, in the southwestern province of Yunnan, who participated in a clash over land development that left nine dead.

2014-10-30.title: Reinforcements Enter Besieged Syrian Town via Turkey, Raising Hopes
2014-10-30.abstract: Officials in Kobani, Syria, hailed the arrival of Iraqi Kurdish soldiers and Syrian rebels, saying they hoped it heralded a more cooperative effort to fight the Islamic State.

2014-10-30.title: Colombia: Army Database Raises Concern About Possible Spying
2014-10-30.abstract: The authorities said they were investigating an allegation that the army kept a database of email addresses for people seeking a peace deal with the country’s main guerrilla group.

2014-10-30.title: After Ukraine Vote, a New Test: Burying a Legacy of Dysfunctional Politics
2014-10-30.abstract: Dysfunction between presidents and prime ministers has hobbled the country, leaving it one of the poorest on the European Continent and often beholden to the whims of Russia.

2014-10-30.title: Troops to Be Checked for Chemical Exposure in Iraq
2014-10-30.abstract: A Pentagon review comes in response to an investigation by The New York Times of how troops who were exposed to nerve and mustard agents were treated by the military.

2014-10-30.title: Secularist Win Is Confirmed in Tunisia
2014-10-30.abstract: The results were a blow for the Islamists who swept to power in the 2011 elections after the revolution that overthrew the government of President Zine el-Abdine Ben Ali.

2014-10-31.title: Russia: Court Rules Against Tycoon
2014-10-31.abstract: A Moscow court on Thursday ruled in favor of nationalizing a Russian billionaire’s majority stake in the oil producer Bashneft, one of the country’s largest private oil companies.

2014-10-31.title: North Korea Said to Impose Ebola Quarantine on All Travelers
2014-10-31.abstract: North Korea, which in the best of times allows a limited number of people in or out, is so rattled by the virus that it reportedly will quarantine anyone coming into the country from anywhere.

2014-10-31.title: Husband’s Arrest in India Tests Colonial-Era Sex Law
2014-10-31.abstract: A man accused of having extramarital sex with men was charged with violating an 1861 law that India’s Supreme Court reinstated last December.

2014-10-31.title: Images of a Dramatic Day in Burkina Faso
2014-10-31.abstract: Video and photographs shared online Thursday showed chaotic celebrations in the capital, Ouagadougou, as protesters toppled the government.

2014-10-31.title: Protest Is Muted as Egypt Levels Border Area in Sinai
2014-10-31.abstract: The demolitions are part of a government plan to create a buffer to curb the flow of weapons and militants between Egypt and the Gaza Strip.
